commit.message:
ping_agent, auth_agent: increase batch size from 500 to 2000

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): rebuild HEMesh after push-pull so face splitting works

After push-pull creates/modifies geometry via Manifold, the HEMesh
becomes stale. Now we rebuild it from the new BufferGeometry so that
line-tool face splitting works on push-pull-created boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): use point-in-face lookup instead of stale triangle index

ensureHEMesh() rebuilds the geometry from the HEMesh, which changes all
triangle indices. The targetFaceIndex captured during pickDrawingPlane
was stale by the time finalizeLine used it.

Fix: add findFaceAtPoint() which does a 3D point-on-plane check + 2D
point-in-polygon test to find the correct HE face from the line's start
point, independent of triangle indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ping_agent: remove status updates, delegate to auth_agent

Ping agent now only records metrics and updates last_ping_at.
Status (online/offline) is managed exclusively by auth_agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add half-edge mesh kernel with face splitting

Implements a custom half-edge data structure (DCEL) as the topological
foundation for SketchUp-like 3D modeling operations.

Phase 1 - Kernel Core:
- HEMesh class with traversal (faceLoop, faceVertices, adjacentFaces) and
  mutation (addFace, splitFace, splitEdge, removeFace)
- Ear-clipping triangulation for concave polygon faces
- BufferGeometry ↔ HEMesh conversion with vertex welding and coplanar
  triangle merging
- JSON serialization/deserialization

Phase 2 - Integration Bridge:
- ensureHEMesh() lazily upgrades CAD objects to use topology
- syncMeshFromHE() regenerates Three.js geometry after mutations
- buildFaceDataFromHE() provides O(1) face lookup vs O(n) coplanar scan
- heMesh persisted alongside geometry in save/load

Phase 3 - Face Splitting:
- findLineFaceIntersection() detects where lines cross face boundaries
- splitFaceByLineSegment() splits faces into independent polygons
- LineTool attempts topological face split before falling back to thin box

54 unit tests passing (39 kernel + 15 face split).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove obsolete version attribute from docker-compose.yml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch build stage from eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine to eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove broken FAQ microdata — rely on JSON-LD for structured data

Accordion answers rendered with {isOpen && ...} were absent from the DOM
on page load, so Google saw Question.name with no acceptedAnswer — the
exact GSC error. Microdata stripped from faq.tsx entirely; the JSON-LD
FAQPage schema in structured-data.tsx already has all 8 Q&A pairs with
proper acceptedAnswer fields and is what Google should be reading.

- Send full-resolution images in ScreenSpot-Pro eval (no 960px downscale)
- Add benchmark scripts: screenspot_pro/eval.py, osworld/detm_agent.py

Smoke test: 36% on first 100 ScreenSpot-Pro samples (Config B),
matching published UI-TARS-1.5-7B baseline of 35.7%.

feat: The Pit + Vol Slayer persona rewrite (#51)

* feat: The Pit — multi-agent dialectical debate engine

Three-round adversarial debate using Claude Agent SDK:
  Round 1: Bull and Bear argue in parallel (independent)
  Round 2: Each reads opponent's argument, delivers rebuttal
  Round 3: Judge synthesizes, scores, delivers verdict

First debate (NVDA overvaluation thesis) surfaced 20 distinct
considerations including the inference/training mix shift that
neither Bull nor Bear identified independently.

Includes:
- Structured consideration counter (replaced broken bullet-count heuristic)
- AgentMemory integration — debate lessons persist for future sessions
- JSON + Markdown transcript saving
- Interactive REPL mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add single-agent baseline for debate experiment validation

3 baseline runs (generic analyst, no persona) vs 3 debates on same questions.
Results: +49% avg consideration uplift from debate format (13.7 → 20.3).
All 3 questions clear the 20% kill condition. Hypothesis confirmed.

- Identity-first opening ("reads the wave before it breaks") replaces job title

- Surfing metaphor for teaching instinct (lead with intuition, then math)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(v16): Phase 1 — server protocol moveAngle/aimAngle split

Implement move/aim heading separation for TPS control preparation:

Server:
- InputPayload: add ma (moveAngle), aa (aimAngle), j (jump) fields with
  backward-compatible fallback (legacy 'a' field maps to both)
- Agent struct: split Heading → MoveHeading + AimHeading, TargetAngle →
  MoveTargetAngle + AimTargetAngle (legacy fields kept in sync)
- ApplyInputSplit + HandleInputSplit pipeline through Arena → Room →
  RoomManager/CountryArenaManager/WorldManager
- UpdateAgent: MoveHeading drives position, AimHeading drives facing
- Weapon system: uses AimHeading for firing direction
- StateAgent: add 'f' (facing = AimHeading) field to serialization
- Bot AI: unchanged (moveAngle = aimAngle via legacy ApplyInput)

Client:
- AgentNetworkData: add optional 'f' (facing) field
- Interpolation: facing angle lerp between server states
- AgentInstances, HeadGroupManager, EyeInstances, EquipmentInstances:
  character rotation uses facing (f ?? h) for aim direction

commit.message:
Aggregate same-timestamp trades and add min trade size filter

Individual CLOB fills within a single order sweep are not separate
price signals. A large market order fills at many book levels in the
same second (e.g., YES=$0.24 to $0.65), but only one trade event
should exist per (timestamp, market). Now aggregates to VWAP before
feeding into the backtester.

Also adds min_trade_size_usd=$100 filter — tiny fills ($16 picking
off a stale limit at $0.22 in a market trading at $0.40) are noise,
not actionable signals.

Results now realistic: IS Sharpe 2.2, 61% win rate, $936 net PnL,
565 trades with 17h avg hold. OOS Sharpe 4.5. Both pass sanity checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix infinite loop in dealFresh causing third-card drill to freeze

The loop always reset to the same shoe position each attempt, so if
the top 4 cards formed a natural the loop hit 300 iterations uselessly,
then the recursive fallback was stuck on the same cards → infinite
recursion. Now advances through the shoe linearly each attempt.

commit.message:
fix(combinator): correct quorum_met in quorum_timeout all-done branch

The quorum_timeout's "all futures done" branch hardcoded
quorum_met: false instead of recalculating like the base quorum
function does. While this branch is unreachable in practice
(either the quorum-met or too-many-failures check catches first),
making it defensively correct keeps it consistent with the
non-timeout quorum and prevents surprises if the logic changes.

Rename Risk Tier to Confidence Tier in Triple Screen display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use weak import flag for sceVideocodec stubs (#20)

Change sceVideocodec psp_extern flags from 0x4001 to 0x4009,
matching sceAudiocodec's flags. The 0x0008 bit marks the import
as "weak", allowing modules to load even when the codec library
hasn't been loaded yet via sceUtilityLoadModule.

Without this fix, any EBOOT that references sceVideocodec functions
fails to load on real PSP hardware because the kernel can't resolve
the import stubs at module load time (the avcodec firmware module
isn't loaded until runtime).

Co-authored-by: AI Agent Bot <ai-agent@localhost>

commit.message:
feat(deps): upgrade openidconnect 3 → 4

openidconnect 4 removes the bundled async_http_client helper and adopts
a type-state pattern for CoreClient endpoint generics. Key changes:

- Add `http_client: reqwest::Client` field to OidcClient; built with
  `Policy::none()` per the OIDC spec (no redirects during token exchange)
- Update ConfiguredCoreClient type alias: device-auth/introspection/
  revocation use EndpointNotSet (not populated by from_provider_metadata);
  token URL and userinfo URL remain EndpointMaybeSet
- exchange_code now returns Result<CodeTokenRequest, _> when token URL is
  EndpointMaybeSet; propagate with map_err before chaining set_pkce_verifier
- new_stub() updated with reqwest::Client::new()

commit.message:
fix: clear localStorage on login to prevent stale data flash

When the app loads, auth.currentUser is null until onAuthStateChanged
fires, so getCustomTemplates() reads from localStorage (anonymous state).
Logged-in users briefly see localStorage content before Firebase data
replaces it.

Fix: on login, immediately clear the localStorage keys and call
rebuildLibrary() so the UI shows an empty (correct) state while
Firestore loads the user's actual data.

No data loss risk: saveCustomTemplates/saveFrecuentPlayers always
write to both localStorage and Firebase, so all user data is safe
in Firestore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs(poe): Protocol.md §5 — authoritative agent spawn model

Locks down the Claude spawn pattern that must be used consistently.
The -p flag silently breaks poe:decision and poe:review because Claude
exits before mid-task stdin writes can land.

Correct model:
- claude --dangerously-skip-permissions (no -p, no CLI prompt arg)
- T+S+K bundle written to stdin pipe immediately after spawn
- stdin pipe stays open for decision resolutions and review injections
- poe:done is the sole completion authority (not exit code)
- watchdog reads node status from SQLite after PTY EOF

Also documents:
- Session ID capture from startup banner for --resume on restart
- Mid-task input delivery format (---\nHuman: {text}\n)
- Skill design constraint: autonomous single-pass, no interactive prompts,
  genuine blockers via poe:decision only

Beads:
- bp6-pdr.8 (P0 bug): remove -p flag, write bundle to stdin pipe
- bp6-pdr.5, bp6-pdr.7 notes: skills must be rewritten for autonomous
  single-pass execution (not interactive conversation)

commit.message:
feat: 多提供商架构 - 支持 DeepSeek 等国产 LLM

架构重构:
- 新增 Provider 协议层 (LLMProvider, ASRProvider)
- 新增 ProviderFactory 工厂类，支持运行时切换提供商
- 实现 OpenRouterProvider 和 DeepSeekProvider

KeychainManager 扩展:
- 新增 API Key 类型: deepSeek, miniMax, zhiPu, kimi, openAI
- 支持多提供商密钥存储

AppState 扩展:
- 新增 llmProvider 和 asrProvider 选择状态
- 配置持久化到 UserDefaults

LLMClient 重构:
- polishText() 支持 providerType 参数
- 使用 ProviderFactory 创建提供商实例

RecordingCoordinator 更新:
- 从 AppState 读取用户选择的提供商
- 动态获取对应提供商的 API Key

SettingsView UI 重构:
- 添加 LLM/ASR 提供商选择器 (Segmented Picker)
- 根据选择动态显示对应 API Key 输入
- 添加 apiKeyField 辅助函数复用 UI 组件

支持的 LLM 提供商:
- OpenRouter (GPT-4o, Claude 等)
- DeepSeek (国产，性价比高)
- MiniMax (待实现)
- 智谱 GLM (待实现)
- Kimi (待实现)

支持的 ASR 提供商:
- ElevenLabs (已有)
- DeepSeek (待实现)
- OpenAI (待实现)
